mnemonics.title: FIFO Creation
mnemonics.content: mkfifo = make + FIFO - Think 'make a FIFO pipe' - similar to making a file but specifically for pipes

mnemonics.title: PIPE_BUF Remember
mnemonics.content: PIPE_BUF = 4096 - Remember as '4K Buffer' - standard page size in memory systems

mnemonics.title: Blocking Order
mnemonics.content: Remember order: READER opens FIRST - Like waiting in queue - first come, first served

acronyms.usage: Data structure principle where the first data written is the first data read
Mechanisms allowing processes to communicate and synchronize
Maximum bytes guaranteed to be written atomically to a pipe or FIFO

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a physical tube/pipe where you drop balls at one end and they come out the other in same order - first ball in is first ball out
Think of a check-in counter at airport - the check-in person (reader) waits until a passenger (writer) arrives, and vice versa
Think of multiple people pouring water into a funnel - small amounts pour through cleanly, large amounts might mix

analogies.analogy: Anonymous pipe is like a telephone call that ends when you hang up. A FIFO is like a public mailbox with an address - anyone can drop letters and anyone with the address can pick them up.
Blocking is like a one-way door - you can't go through until someone opens the door from the other side.
Writing less than PIPE_BUF is like writing your name on a single sticky note - it won't get mixed with others. Writing more is like trying to write multiple notes at once - they might get mixed up.
